Magical Attraction Practices with Kurukulla, Hayagriva, and Mahadeva.
Seminar via Zoom,  or in person  Saturday and Sunday, time 16.00-20.00 CET with breaks.
The alluring and enchanting Wisdom Dakini known as Kurukulla, who represents the red form
of the female Buddha Tara, was said to come to us originally from the mysterious land of the
Dakinis, Uddiyana, situated in the western dierction. Although embodying the femine aspect of
Buddha enlightenment, this Wisdom Dakini is associated especially with enchantment,
eroticism, and witchcraft, and in particular she uses her seductive and magnetically attracting
powers in order to influence and benefit sentient beings, including buman society and the natural
environment. She is assisted in these activities by her lover and consort, the Padma Heruka
Hayagriva and also by the worldly Guardians known as Mahadeva and Umadevi. The focus of
this seminar will be on the megical attraction practices connected with Kurukulla, as well as with
Hayagriva, Mahadeva, and Umadevi.
https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/dRL9FBCdRN-5IsDeP9H-oQ#/registration

Shitro Ngakso and Neydren Retreat
✨
June 11–13, 2026
Pema Osel Ling & online
Join us for Shitro Ngakso, a retreat centered on the One Hundred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities, with practices of purification, confession, restoration of samaya, and dedication for the deceased.
The retreat begins with the Shitro Empowerment on June 11 and continues with repeated Shitro practice sessions, Ngondro, Riwo Sangchö, tsok, self-empowerment, and the Neydren ceremony.
🌺
Shitro & Neydren Ceremony
The Neydren ceremony is dedicated to beings who have died and are wandering in the bardo. Names of the deceased may be submitted for inclusion in the practice, and participants may also sponsor tsok and butterlamps as offerings during the retreat.
Shitro practice is understood as a profound support for all present and future bardos. At the time of death, when body and mind separate, the mandala of the peaceful and wrathful deities appears. Recognizing these appearances as one’s own wisdom display is said to lead to liberation.
📍
In person at Pema Osel Ling
🎥
Online by livestream

The Windhorse of Blessings: Understanding the Sacred Purpose of Lungta and Dar-chog
Teachings with Venerable Geshema Yungdung Tsultrim Sangmo
With heartfelt joy and devotion, Niu Pal Gyi De Nai is pleased to announce a special weekend teaching on the sacred meaning and purpose of Lungta and Dar-chog.
In the Bön and Tibetan spiritual tradition, Lungta, the “Windhorse,” is deeply connected with life-force, good fortune, vitality, positive energy, and auspicious circumstances. Dar-chog, the sacred victory banner, is raised to invoke blessings, protection, harmony, and the flourishing of virtue and spiritual practice.
These sacred flags are not merely outer symbols. When raised with pure intention, prayer, and devotion, they become a powerful expression of aspiration — carrying blessings through the wind for the benefit of beings, the environment, and all directions.
During this teaching, Venerable Geshema Yungdung Tsultrim Sangmo will kindly explain the deeper meaning, purpose, and benefits of raising Lungta and Dar-chog, and how practitioners can understand this beautiful tradition in a meaningful and spiritual way.

Venerable Geshema Yungdung Tsultrim Sangmo

✨
Chaphur Rinpoche back in Poland - September 2026
✨
We are very happy to invite you for another meeting with Chaphur Rinpoche.
In September 2026, we will have the unique opportunity to continue our practice together during two events:
📅
September 3-4, 2026
🪷
Keksel Teaching Course - Part II
📅
September 5-6, 2026
🔥
The teachings and initiation of Takla Mebar
🪷
Keksel is a unique system of body, breath and energy work that combines elements of tantra and dzogczen. Through movement, breath work and subtle energies it helps to harmonize body and mind, release accumulated tensions and transform negative emotions into wisdom. It's a practice that supports not only spiritual growth but also health, balance and inner peace.
🔥
On September 5-6, Chaphur Rinpoche will give the teachings and initiations of Takla Mebar, a deep healing practice of the Yungdrung Bon tradition. Takla Mebars, meaning the "Burning Tiger Deity," is a powerful method for clearing obstacles, negative energies, and fears. This practice helps to awaken inner strength, mental clarity, and the ability to transform difficult emotions into five wisdoms.
Although the roots of this practice go back to ancient Tantric teachings, Rinpoche will impart it in an affordable and practical way, so that everyone can benefit from its benefits in their daily lives as well.
🌿
Whether you are continuing your Keksel studies or just beginning your Bon journey, this will be a unique opportunity to receive one-on-one guidance from Chaphura Rinpoche, deepen your path, and meet a community of practitioners.
Detailed information regarding the venue of the event and registration will be provided soon.
We cordially invite you!
🙏
Chaphur Foundation Poland

text:
Nam Chö Tsa Lung Tummo Preliminaries
– with Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche
Starting on Saturday, June 13, 2026 (further dates below)
5:00 pm – 6:00 pm CET, 11:00 pm – 12:00 am MYT
Language: English with simultaneous translation into German and Russian
Contribution: 40 – 120 € for the whole course
Everyone is welcome! No previous knowledge required.
Registration:
https://www.eventbrite.de/e/tummo-preliminaries-tickets-1990484573943
Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche will begin teaching the Tummo Preliminaries, the essential foundation practices for the authentic Tummo tradition.
These preliminary practices cultivate self-love, compassion, positive qualities, purification, and meditation on the nature of reality. They provide the basis for the deeper practices of the Nam Chö Tsa Lung Tummo cycle.
This is a particularly special opportunity, as Rinpoche intends to teach the entire Tummo cycle within just ten months. Participants who complete the Preliminaries will be prepared to receive the Tummo empowerment in Berlin this autumn and continue with the main Tummo practices.
https://www.dolpotulku.org/en/termin/tummo-retreat-empowerment/
Further dates: Mon 22.06. | Mon 6.07. | Mon 20.07. | Mon 27.07. | Mon 3.08. | Mon 10.08. | Mon 31.08. | Mon 7.09. | Mon 14.09.
Authentic Tummo teachings are rarely available and are traditionally transmitted step by step. If you have an interest in meditation, yoga, the subtle energy body, or the Vajrayana path, this is an excellent opportunity to begin the complete Tummo journey from the very beginning.
We warmly invite you to join us and take the first step on this profound path.
You can find more information on our website:
https://www.dolpotulku.org/en/termin/nam-choe-tsa-lung-tummo-preliminaries-15/

text:
Foundations of Tibetan Yoga: Tsalung and Lujong Intensive
Dr. James Bae is offering a foundational program in Tibetan Yoga, which occurs in 3-5 day intensives. These consist of Lujong – one of three principle yogic exercise systems taught to train the body – and tsalung trulkhor – which work more explicitly on the channels, winds and vital essences and operate within the Completion Phase.
In addition, an overview of Tibetan Yoga and an introduction to breathing methods such as the vase or ‘bumpachen’ and the progression of practice, is provided. Dr. James also introduces topics such as wind obstacles – a particular specialization – to provide cautionary guidelines for continued practice.
This program has developed from over five years of public training programs and several cohorts. There is no prerequisite for empowerment, and the general public is invited to these thorough introductory trainings.
All of these traditional systems are held within distinct lineages and are taught with authorization/permission. Following, interested parties may continue with a more extensive cycle of tsalung, following empowerment, preliminary and generation stage practice (i.e. Dudjom Tersar).
Foundations of Tibetan Yoga intensives provide an opportunity for dharma practitioners and general populations training in traditional Buddhist yoga systems, to support their primary lineage practice, as well as an immersion into physical or energetic practices for health and to support meditation.
James Bae offers these programs in-person only. These programs are offered with a suggestion donation scale, and amenable to sangha needs as per location.
We are offering two upcoming programs:
Garchen Meditation Center, June 24-26
France, Sept 4-6. Donation plus the center’s lodging and meal costs (residential or commuter). Near Montpellier

In this four days divided into two weekends, only on Zoom, Geshe Choekhortshang Rinpoche will bestow the continuation of the preliminaries (started in 2025) for the Tummo practice from Ma Gyu and Kusum Rangshar lineages. text:
Join us for a rare and profound three-day Dzogchen retreat in the sacred Valley of the Dakinis, Lincoln, Vermont.
The Drikung Dzogchen Community of Vermont warmly invites you to The Completion of Primordial Wisdom: A Yangzab Dzogchen Retreat with Ven. Khenpo Yeshi Rinpoche — a deeply trained scholar-practitioner whose path includes intensive Dzogchen retreat, decades of teaching in Nepal and the U.S., and a long connection with His Eminence Garchen Rinpoche.
The Yangzab treasure teachings are unique to the Drikung Kagyü lineage, revealed as an earth treasure by Drikung Tertön Rinchen Phuntsok.
Padmasambhava himself prophesied that these teachings would be especially effective at cutting through spiritual darkness in difficult times — swift and complete in leading to full realization.
✨
Over three days, Khenpo Rinpoche will guide us through The Completion of Primordial Wisdom, the root Dzogrim text of the Yangzab cycle, along with Padmasattva Guru Yoga and transmissions for the completion-stage practices of Trekchö and Thögal.
🏔
A holy place of practice. His Holiness Drikung Chetsang Rinpoche and other lamas have recognized this valley in Lincoln as a true power place for spiritual practice — a sacred land where the dakinis abide. It was here that His Holiness had a Peace Stupa built, and ever since His Holiness the 16th Karmapa first visited in 1977, well over thirty Tibetan teachers have come to this valley to turn the wheel of Dharma.
This year, the Drikung Dzogchen Community of Vermont celebrates its 25th anniversary of holding the Yangzab lineage here. Come help us mark this milestone in such a holy and blessed place.
🙏
Whether you join us in person at Sunray Peace Village or online from anywhere in the world, this is a rare opportunity to connect with an authentic living lineage in a land long devoted to awakening.
Our Vision
🌿
DDCV was envisioned as a bridge between traditions — preserving the integrity of each lineage while creating a living environment for practice, study, and realization in the 21st century, as held by the supreme Dharma lord Drikung Rinchen Phuntsok (1509–1557).
Our path weaves three streams into one: the Yangzab Dzogchen treasure teachings (the natural perfection of awareness), Drikung Kagyü Mahāmudrā (direct realization of the nature of mind), and Kadampa Lojong (mind-training and compassion in daily life). Through these, practitioners purify obscurations, open wisdom, and awaken uncontrived compassion.
"The DDCV stands for the inseparable union of view and conduct—where Dzogchen's vastness, Mahamudra's clarity, and Kadampa's discipline meet in one stream of living Dharma."
— H.E. Ontul Rinpoche, founder of DDCV

About the Teacher
Ven. Khenpo Yeshi Rinpoche was born in Nakchu, East Tibet, in 1969. He showed a deep devotion to the Dharma from early childhood, and at thirteen received empowerments, transmissions, and oral instructions in the Longchen Nyingthig cycle from His Eminence Lungtok Rinpoche and His Eminence Nidrag Khenpo Konchok Gyaltsen.
At twenty he traveled to South India, where he trained across the Geluk, Kagyü, and Nyingma traditions at Sera Monastery, Dzogchen Monastery, and Drikung Jangchup Ling, excelling in both sutra and tantra.

He studied closely with more than two dozen eminent masters and received the kama and terma transmissions of the Nyingma tradition from teachers including His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama, Tulku Urgyen Rinpoche, Chatral Sangye Dorje Rinpoche, and His Holiness Drikung Chetsang Rinpoche.
In 1993 he entered an intensive three-year retreat in Nepal under the renowned Dzogchen master Tulku Urgyen Rinpoche, who granted him special Dzogchen teachings and personal instructions at the Asura Cave Retreat Center in Pharping. He went on to teach at Ka-Nying Shedrup Ling and Rangjung Yeshe Institute, helping shape monastic and international curricula still in use today. His connection with His Eminence Garchen Rinpoche, beginning in 1998, eventually brought him to reside in the United States.
Dr. Khenpo Rinpoche holds a PhD in South and Southeast Asian Studies from UC Berkeley and is currently a postdoctoral fellow at the Ho Center for Buddhist Studies at Stanford University.
The combination of his deep retreat experience, breadth of lineage transmission, and scholarly depth makes the chance to receive these teachings directly from him a genuinely rare opportunity.

Dear friends,
Here is the recording of Chris Jay’s heartfelt and inspiring class last Saturday:
https://youtu.be/KD0qe6MjlCA?si=b9XGf4JviJft0PDG
And we are delighted to share that this coming Saturday, June 20, 7 pm IST the incomparable *Chagdud Khadro* will teach us how to “experience emptiness.”  *You are welcome to invite friends to this special teaching by sharing the Zoom link*:
https://us06web.zoom.us/j/88630561198?pwd=YdF6oLNs7L3LqFbC3XGkxaO8lk38xW.1
_Chagdud Khadro is the spiritual director of Chagdud Gonpa Brasil, Uruguay, and Chile, and head of Khadro Ling – the first Tibetan Buddhist centre in South America. Married for 23 years to Chagdud Rinpoche, she now upholds the spiritual lineages that he transmitted, is widely known for her teachings on preparing for death, and is passionate about imparting the dharma to children. In 2024, Chagdud Khadro was awarded the Khyentse Fellowship, Khyentse Foundation’s top honour, for her tireless contributions and commitment to the Buddhadharma._

text:
Seven Treasures Retreat begins tomorrow, June 15, at Pema Osel Ling
Join us June 15–24 for the Collection of Seven Treasures Guru Rinpoche Retreat with H.E. Namgyal Dawa Rinpoche.
The retreat begins tomorrow with the Seven Treasures Empowerment, followed in the afternoon by a welcoming procession for H.E. Namgyal Dawa Rinpoche. Everyone is warmly invited to gather as we welcome Rinpoche back to Pema Osel Ling and begin this auspicious cycle of practice together.
Over the course of the retreat, participants will join in empowerment, oral transmissions, group practice, tsok, jinsek fire pujas, sacred dance, and the culmination of the retreat with siddhis and sacred dances on the final day.
🔥
Jinsek fire pujas are ritual offering practices using the fire element to accomplish enlightened activity swiftly and powerfully.
🌺
Sacred dance, ritual music, offerings, and repeated practice come together as part of this profound Vajrayana retreat centered on Guru Rinpoche and the blessings of the lineage.
All are welcome to attend in person or online. No one will be turned away due to lack of funds. Please contact office@vajrayana.org if you need assistance to attend.

Nepal Dharma Programs with Drupön Lama Karma Rinpoche – Spring 2027
We invite you to join Drupön Lama Karma Rinpoche in Nepal for a series of retreats and pilgrimages at sacred places associated with Guru Rinpoche.
📍
Pharping Retreat
February 27 – March 8, 2027
A 10-day retreat in Pharping with daily teachings, meditation practice, group practice and Q&A
📍
Maratika Amitayus Retreat with Yarinak Pilgrimage
March 13 – 25, 2027
Pilgrimage and practice in Yarinak, followed by an Amitayus retreat in Maratika. The retreat will include teachings and practice on Amitayus from Tertön Pegyal Lingpa’s tradition.
📍
Muktinath (Chumig Gyatsa) Pilgrimage
April 1 – 7, 2027
A pilgrimage to Chumig Gyatsa (Muktinath), one of the most sacred sites connected with Guru Rinpoche with Lama-la.
Special Note
Drupön Rinpoche will bestow Tertön Pegyal Lingpa's Seven-Line Prayer Empowerment during both the Pharping Retreat and the Maratika Amitayus Retreat. This is a rare and auspicious opportunity, as Lama-la holds the warm blessing and direct transmission of this teaching from Tertön Pegyal Lingpa

About the Teacher:
Bhikkhuni 𝐀𝐲𝐲𝐚 𝐊𝐚̄𝐫𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐤𝐚̄ is currently the Abbot at Santi Forest Monastery, NSW, Australia and the spiritual director for New Zealand Bhikkhuni Sangha Trust, Hamilton. Ayya Karunika started her monastic life in 2010 and she received her full ordination as a bhikkhunī in 2014 at Dhammasara Nuns monastery in Western Australia where she lived and trained for over a decade with Ajahn Hāsapaññā. She has been a student of Ajahn Brahm and Ajahn Brahmali for over 2 decades.
Ayya Kārunikā is able to articulate the Buddhist teachings in a way that is practical and comprehensible to people of all ages and has experience in conducting retreats for both adults and youth. While she has a PhD in Microbiology and worked as a scientist, she has also been the building project manager for the construction of the Dhammasara Nuns Monastery main building complex and has worked with many volunteers over the years. Ayya Kārunikā was born in Sri Lanka but has been living and working in Australia for over 2 decades.
She has a passion to share her experience and knowledge and loves working with people and also doing creative projects. She is dedicated to supporting the growth of Bhikkhunis around the world and is currently involved in projects to support the establishment of places for Bhikkhunis in Theravada forest tradition in Australia, New Zealand, Poland and Sri Lanka.

10th – 11th & 17th–18th, OCTOBER 2026
In this four days divided into two weekends, only on Zoom, Geshe Choekhortshang Rinpoche will bestow the continuation of the preliminaries (started in 2025) for the Tummo practice.
We will experience the preparation retreat to achieve the basic level for the next part of Tummo practice in 2027.
Tummo practice is a very powerful, secret and ancient yogic practice which will allow you to use the inner heat to burn our karmic traces, our illness, our emotional afflictions and increase our body and mental well-being, our mental clarity, and our open-heartendness. karmic winds vs wisdom winds

𝐒𝐲𝐧𝐨𝐩𝐬𝐢𝐬: What you cling to will sink you, no matter how beautiful it appears. Let go, bail out the boat, and cross to the far shore by cultivating mindfulness in daily life (based on Snp. 4.1 𝐾𝑎𝑚𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑡𝑡𝑎 and Snp 5.1 𝐴𝑗𝑖𝑡ℎ𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑡𝑡𝑎)

Join us on Wednesday, June 24th for "Wandering and Meditating in Wild Places" by Dr. Julia Stenzel
The Dakinis’ Vast Resounding Expanse (mkha’ ’gro gad rgyangs) is the chöd practice of the Khandro Nyingthik tradition, revealed by the tertön Jigme Lingpa (1730–1798). This practice forms the basis for meditation in wild and liminal environments, known as nyensa (gnyan sa), a peripatetic meditation tradition in which practitioners are constantly on the move and meditate in caves, charnel grounds, and other traditionally consecrated or haunted landscapes. For a meditator on nyensa, the landscape itself becomes an active field of practice as opposed to the experience in a single bounded retreat site. Drawing on the 108-day nyensa practice of Khentrul Ngawang Lhundrup, a disciple of Chatral Rinpoche, in the spring of 2025, this lecture will first introduce three key texts that form the commentarial basis of mkha’ ‘gro gad rgyang. It will then present observations and reflections from Khentrul Ngawang Lhundrup’s journey, highlighting how doctrinal instructions are enacted through movement across diverse environments. Finally, it will address a broader question: What significance does meditative wandering through wilderness, charnel grounds, and other liminal or fear-inducing spaces hold for Buddhist practitioners in the twenty-first century?
Dr. Julia Stenzel is director of studies and associate professor at Rangjung Yeshe Institute, Kathmandu. She received her doctorate in Buddhist Studies from McGill University in Montreal, Canada. Her research examines ethics in Indian and Tibetan Mahāyāna Buddhism. Her doctoral research examined Buddhist compassion cultivation and its adaptation in secular compassion training programs.

text:
Dzogchen Mumed Dalpa
with Khenchen Tenpa Yungdrung Rinpoche
July 17 to August 5, 2026
In Shenten Dargye Ling and online
The Mume Dalpa is a foundational Dzogchen text within the Yungdrung Bön tradition. It presents the direct experience of the mind’s natural, non-conceptual state — and is beyond all intellectual thought. The term Mu-med means “beyond concept or duality,” and bdal-pa refers to vast "all-pervasiveness" or as well in terms as "primordial or pure awareness" (Rigpa).
This year’s Summer Retreat at Shenten Dargye Ling is guided by Khenchen Tenpa Yungdrung Rinpoche, who will continue teaching the profound Dzogchen text Mume Dalpa (Tib. Mu-med bdal-pa).
Following the inspiring beginning of this study and practice last year, we now continue our exploration of these essential teachings. New participants are most welcome. Rinpoche’s clear explanations and compassionate guidance make these teachings accessible to all sincere practitioners, whether familiar with Dzogchen or new to it.
The Mume Dalpa is a foundational Dzogchen text within the Yungdrung Bön tradition. It presents the direct experience of the mind’s natural, non-conceptual state – and is beyond all intellectual thought. The term Mu-med means “beyond concept or duality,” and bdal-pa refers to vast "all-pervasiveness" or, in terms of "primordial or pure awareness" (Rigpa).
During this retreat, participants will receive its teachings and, through meditations, be guided to recognise the essence of Dzogchen (the Great Perfection) in accordance with the Yungdrung Bön tradition.

We are delighted to announce a very special event hosted by Rangjung Yeshe Gomde Pyrénées onsite and online:
Teachings by Kyabjé Khen Rinpoche Tashi Palden
(Kyabjé Khenpo)
On Heart Sutra
25th July to 3rd August 2026 -  11am CET
Onsite and Online
The Heart Sutra, Sanskrit Prajnaparamitahridaya-sutra (“Discourse on the Heart of the Perfection of Wisdom”), in Mahayana Buddhism, an extremely brief yet highly influential distillation of the essence of (“Perfection of Wisdom”) writings.

It is both rare and precious for a master like His Eminence Jhado Rinpoche to come to the UK and turn the wheel of Dharma.
This September, at the request of his students here and as part of his wider European tour, Rinpoche comes to London for five days of teaching, from Wednesday 9 to Sunday 13 September.
It is a programme of depth, moving through the breadth of the Tibetan Buddhist path:
Days 1 & 2 (9–10 Sept): Kalachakra initiation, with teachings on the Three Principal Aspects of the Path and Lojong (mind training)
Day 3 (11 Sept): Cittamani Tara initiation and oral transmission
Days 4 & 5 (12–13 Sept): Lama Tsongkhapa and Medicine Buddha jenangs, with commentary on the Lama Chöpa (Guru Puja)
The programme is designed so students and practitioners can attend all five days, or you can select:
- days 1 and 2 together
- just day 3
- days 4 and 5 together
- any combination thereof
Rinpoche's UK visit is hosted in conjunction with our friends at Jamyang Leeds and Land of Joy.
Registration opens Wednesday 1 July. Hold the dates, and we will share the booking link as soon as it is live; the chance to sit with a teacher of this calibre is not one that comes around often.
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdsbLFzb6X7bi_to-73gXX80s2Aue40YgacjXZ8NuRBg5aqQg/viewform

𝐀𝐲𝐲𝐚 𝐒𝐞𝐫𝐢, who entered the monastic life as an anāgārika in 2002, received her Bhikkhuni Ordination in 2009 at Bodhinyana Monastery at Western Australia. Ayya Seri, together with her teacher Ayya Vayama Bhikkhuni Theri, founded Patacara Bhikkhuni Hermitage in Perth, Western Australia in 2011. text:
Online Gegsel Retreat
– with Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche
Yoga from the Sky Dharma Tradition
Friday, July 3, 2026 – Sunday, July 5, 2026
Each day
5:00 pm – 8:00 pm CET, 11:00 pm – 2:00 am MYT
Language: English with simultaneous German and Russian translation
Contribution: 60 €
No previous knowledge is required, everyone is welcome!
Registration:
https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_yVMddQ6HSk6mcgPs_-m8Uw#/registration
After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the webinar. If you do not have a Paypal account, please register with Carmen (carmen@dolpotulku.org) and transfer the fee to our association account.
We warmly invite you to join Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche for the Nam Chö Tsa Lung Gegsel Online Retreat and discover an authentic yoga practice from the Nam Chö (Sky Dharma) tradition.
Gegsel, meaning "overcoming obstacles," combines movement, breath control, visualization, and meditation to release physical tension, emotional stress, and energetic blockages while cultivating greater balance, vitality, and inner clarity. The Namchö Tsa Lung Gegsel exercises have been passed down in unbroken lineage from Tertön (treasure finder) Tulku Mingyur Dorje (1645–1667) to Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche.
Although Gegsel is traditionally taught alongside Tummo practice, no previous experience is required. Whether you are completely new to Tibetan yoga or already have an established meditation practice, this retreat offers a wonderful opportunity to experience these profound methods in an accessible and practical way.
Schedule each day:
5:00 pm – 6:30 pm CET teaching and practice by Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche
7:00 pm – 8:00 pm CET practice tutorial
You can find more information about Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche's teachings and activities on our website:
https://www.dolpotulku.org/en/program/

Q&A with Lopon Drukdra Gyatso
*Retreat Master, Gangteng Monastery*
Lopon Drukdra Gyatso serves as Retreat Master of Kinzang Choling Three-Year Retreat Centre — the principal retreat centre for graduates of Gangteng Buddhist University, under the guidance of His Holiness Gangteng Rinpoche. In this role, he oversees and guides practitioners through structured retreat training and advanced meditation practice. He also teaches Vajrayana and tantric subjects to Shedra students of Grades 7–9, providing direct guidance in tantric yoga and related contemplative practices, bridging theoretical understanding with correct application.
For all practitioners of the Kunsang Gondu ngöndro — a special opportunity to have your questions answered directly by Lopon Drukdra Gyatso.

text:
Gosia is offering an ongoing series of Tummo classes with a particular emphasis on an embodied and intuitive approach to Highest Yoga Tantra. This will be a safe and nurturing space — especially welcoming to female practitioners — for those who wish to explore a more experiential and embodied style of HYT practice, encompassing both the Generation and Completion Stages.
📅
Beginning on Friday, July 10th, at 3pm Eastern Time / 8pm UK time, we will gather every week at the same day and time for guided practice followed by Q&A. This new cycle will focus on Vajrayogini Generation Stage practice alongside the Completion Stage practices of the Six Yogas of Naropa, and will have a rich, community-centred format that encourages connection and mutual support.
⏺️
Sessions can be attended live or accessed through recordings, depending on what your week allows.
🫂
The heart of this offering is the creation of a community of intuitive practitioners who walk the path together — supporting one another on the journey toward Enlightenment for the benefit of all beings. We each carry unique gifts to share, and it is in that spirit of open exchange that wisdom, compassion, and genuine breakthroughs arise naturally, held within an atmosphere of kindness and mutual respect.
🧘‍♀️
🧘
Looking forward to seeing you in the Dakini Land community.
🙏
You can register here:
https://dakini-land.circle.so/checkout/dakini-tummo
📿
📿
📿
About Gosia:
✨
Gosia has been a dedicated practitioner of Vajrayana Buddhism for twenty years, and is based in the south-west of Ireland. A sociologist and psychotherapist by training, she brings a deep passion for discovering and expanding human potential — both within herself and in those she works with.
🙏
Gosia has received the transmission and authorisation from her teachers  — Lama Jhampa Shaneman and Lama Glenn Mullin — to teach and transmit Tantra. This includes the Tummo practices of the Six Yogas of Naropa and Niguma, Chittamani Tara, the Amitayus Healing Tummo, and a range of other Vajrayana practices.
See you soon,
🪷
Gosia

text:
Due to several requests, I will give the lung/oral transmission and tri/commentary for Dudjom Rinpoche's very special, extremely short ngon-dro on the most holy day of Cho-kor Du-chen:
https://www.lotsawahouse.org/tibetan-masters/dudjom-rinpoche/key-summary-ngondro-main?fbclid=IwY2xjawS0pj5leHRuA2FlbQIxMQBzcnRjBmFwcF9pZBAyMjIwMzkxNzg4MjAwODkyAAEe-u-Y8RudgX6eafXvjMFJqHotIb1TPBEw-cAxe1eNHd9NnzH658vM6zhpD6A_aem_9l7UIAwjt-yWodftynvY0Q
This ngon-dro was requested from Kyabje Dudjom Rinpoche by Jetsun Pema Chodzin Sangngag Wangmo, the reincarnation of Jetsun Shugseb Chonyid Wangmo, when Jetsun Pema Rinpoche and Her Family, Mr. & Mrs. Sonam T. Kazi, were preparing to move to NYC to teach a group of Western students in 1970. In the colophon, it says that this ngon-dro was specifically composed for busy modern people. As written, it does not include prostrations, just the five accumulations of refuge, Bodhicitta, Vajrasattva, mandala offering, and Vajra Guru mantra. In retreat, these accumulations can be done in 15-21 days. I received this ngon-dro from Mr. Kazi in Delhi in 1970 and again from Kyabje Dudjom Rinpoche in 1972. I've been doing this ngon-dro for 56 years.
Date: Saturday, July 18, 8 AM MDT
Duration: 90 minutes
Join Zoom Meeting
https://us02web.zoom.us/j/83278902405?pwd=ktXgHGbCqZmoDa9U4uCoVSbc9KIF6y.1
Meeting chat link
https://us02web.zoom.us/launch/jc/83278902405
Meeting ID: 832 7890 2405
Passcode: 341439
Prerequisite: The samaya or promise to complete 111,111 recitations of each of the five accumulations within a maximum of one year from the date of the transmission.
This transmission is only for those who seriously want to practice this ngon-dro. It is not for Dharma "stamp-collectors." Please do not attend this transmission unless you are ready and willing to accept this samaya.
Dana: The suggested minimum thanksgiving offering is $100.  This is about Enlightenment, not some worldly benefit. As such, this sadhana is incredibly precious. An offering of $1000, $10,000, $100,000, or even $1,000,000 would not be too much if one understands its true value. I spent six months traveling back and forth to India overland twice in order to receive this sadhana.  All offerings for this lung will, in turn, be offered to the building of the Vajrakilaya Peace Mandala at Phurba Thrinley Ling above the Mississippi River in Lansing, IA. This has nothing to do with me or my economy. It has to do with how precious this very special ngon-dro is.
Offerings may be made via Paypal:
@lamapc13
or Venmo:
@lamapema13
Recipients of this transmission may ask me on-going questions about its practice by emailing me at: 13pemachophel@gmail.com. Depending on interest and need, I am open to scheduling further Zoom classes on instructions on this practice. I make the commitment to helping those who receive this ngon-dro to complete it in whatever ways I can.
After this transmission is complete, those who have received it may email me for Mr. Kazi's and my translation of it.

text:
Magical Attraction Practices with Kurukulla, Hayagriva, and Mahadeva.
Seminar via Zoom,  or in person  Saturday and Sunday, time 16.00-20.00 CET with breaks.
The alluring and enchanting Wisdom Dakini known as Kurukulla, who represents the red form
of the female Buddha Tara, was said to come to us originally from the mysterious land of the
Dakinis, Uddiyana, situated in the western dierction. Although embodying the femine aspect of
Buddha enlightenment, this Wisdom Dakini is associated especially with enchantment,
eroticism, and witchcraft, and in particular she uses her seductive and magnetically attracting
powers in order to influence and benefit sentient beings, including buman society and the natural
environment. She is assisted in these activities by her lover and consort, the Padma Heruka
Hayagriva and also by the worldly Guardians known as Mahadeva and Umadevi. The focus of
this seminar will be on the megical attraction practices connected with Kurukulla, as well as with
Hayagriva, Mahadeva, and Umadevi.

text:
Shitro Ngakso and Neydren Retreat
✨
June 11–13, 2026
Pema Osel Ling & online
Join us for Shitro Ngakso, a retreat centered on the One Hundred Peaceful and Wrathful Deities, with practices of purification, confession, restoration of samaya, and dedication for the deceased.
The retreat begins with the Shitro Empowerment on June 11 and continues with repeated Shitro practice sessions, Ngondro, Riwo Sangchö, tsok, self-empowerment, and the Neydren ceremony.
🌺
Shitro & Neydren Ceremony
The Neydren ceremony is dedicated to beings who have died and are wandering in the bardo. Names of the deceased may be submitted for inclusion in the practice, and participants may also sponsor tsok and butterlamps as offerings during the retreat.
Shitro practice is understood as a profound support for all present and future bardos. At the time of death, when body and mind separate, the mandala of the peaceful and wrathful deities appears. Recognizing these appearances as one’s own wisdom display is said to lead to liberation.
📍
In person at Pema Osel Ling
🎥
Online by livestream
🔗

The Windhorse of Blessings: Understanding the Sacred Purpose of Lungta and Dar-chog
Teachings with Venerable Geshema Yungdung Tsultrim Sangmo
With heartfelt joy and devotion, Niu Pal Gyi De Nai is pleased to announce a special weekend teaching on the sacred meaning and purpose of Lungta and Dar-chog.
In the Bön and Tibetan spiritual tradition, Lungta, the “Windhorse,” is deeply connected with life-force, good fortune, vitality, positive energy, and auspicious circumstances. Dar-chog, the sacred victory banner, is raised to invoke blessings, protection, harmony, and the flourishing of virtue and spiritual practice.
These sacred flags are not merely outer symbols. When raised with pure intention, prayer, and devotion, they become a powerful expression of aspiration — carrying blessings through the wind for the benefit of beings, the environment, and all directions.
During this teaching, Venerable Geshema Yungdung Tsultrim Sangmo will kindly explain the deeper meaning, purpose, and benefits of raising Lungta and Dar-chog, and how practitioners can understand this beautiful tradition in a meaningful and spiritual way.
Bestowed by:
Venerable Geshema Yungdung Tsultrim Sangmo

✨
Chaphur Rinpoche back in Poland - September 2026
✨
We are very happy to invite you for another meeting with Chaphur Rinpoche.
In September 2026, we will have the unique opportunity to continue our practice together during two events:
📅
September 3-4, 2026
🪷
Keksel Teaching Course - Part II
📅
September 5-6, 2026
🔥
The teachings and initiation of Takla Mebar
🪷
Keksel is a unique system of body, breath and energy work that combines elements of tantra and dzogczen. Through movement, breath work and subtle energies it helps to harmonize body and mind, release accumulated tensions and transform negative emotions into wisdom. It's a practice that supports not only spiritual growth but also health, balance and inner peace.
🔥
On September 5-6, Chaphur Rinpoche will give the teachings and initiations of Takla Mebar, a deep healing practice of the Yungdrung Bon tradition. Takla Mebars, meaning the "Burning Tiger Deity," is a powerful method for clearing obstacles, negative energies, and fears. This practice helps to awaken inner strength, mental clarity, and the ability to transform difficult emotions into five wisdoms.
Although the roots of this practice go back to ancient Tantric teachings, Rinpoche will impart it in an affordable and practical way, so that everyone can benefit from its benefits in their daily lives as well.
🌿
Whether you are continuing your Keksel studies or just beginning your Bon journey, this will be a unique opportunity to receive one-on-one guidance from Chaphura Rinpoche, deepen your path, and meet a community of practitioners.
Detailed information regarding the venue of the event and registration will be provided soon.
We cordially invite you!
🙏
Chaphur Foundation Poland

Nam Chö Tsa Lung Tummo Preliminaries
– with Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche
Starting on Saturday, June 13, 2026 (further dates below)
5:00 pm – 6:00 pm CET, 11:00 pm – 12:00 am MYT
Language: English with simultaneous translation into German and Russian
Contribution: 40 – 120 € for the whole course
Everyone is welcome! No previous knowledge required.
Registration:
https://www.eventbrite.de/e/tummo-preliminaries-tickets-1990484573943
Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche will begin teaching the Tummo Preliminaries, the essential foundation practices for the authentic Tummo tradition.
These preliminary practices cultivate self-love, compassion, positive qualities, purification, and meditation on the nature of reality. They provide the basis for the deeper practices of the Nam Chö Tsa Lung Tummo cycle.
This is a particularly special opportunity, as Rinpoche intends to teach the entire Tummo cycle within just ten months. Participants who complete the Preliminaries will be prepared to receive the Tummo empowerment in Berlin this autumn and continue with the main Tummo practices.
https://www.dolpotulku.org/en/termin/tummo-retreat-empowerment/
Further dates: Mon 22.06. | Mon 6.07. | Mon 20.07. | Mon 27.07. | Mon 3.08. | Mon 10.08. | Mon 31.08. | Mon 7.09. | Mon 14.09.
Authentic Tummo teachings are rarely available and are traditionally transmitted step by step. If you have an interest in meditation, yoga, the subtle energy body, or the Vajrayana path, this is an excellent opportunity to begin the complete Tummo journey from the very beginning.
We warmly invite you to join us and take the first step on this profound path.
You can find more information on our website:
https://www.dolpotulku.org/en/termin/nam-choe-tsa-lung-tummo-preliminaries-15/

text:
Foundations of Tibetan Yoga: Tsalung and Lujong Intensive
Dr. James Bae is offering a foundational program in Tibetan Yoga, which occurs in 3-5 day intensives. These consist of Lujong – one of three principle yogic exercise systems taught to train the body – and tsalung trulkhor – which work more explicitly on the channels, winds and vital essences and operate within the Completion Phase.
In addition, an overview of Tibetan Yoga and an introduction to breathing methods such as the vase or ‘bumpachen’ and the progression of practice, is provided. Dr. James also introduces topics such as wind obstacles – a particular specialization – to provide cautionary guidelines for continued practice.
This program has developed from over five years of public training programs and several cohorts. There is no prerequisite for empowerment, and the general public is invited to these thorough introductory trainings.
All of these traditional systems are held within distinct lineages and are taught with authorization/permission. Following, interested parties may continue with a more extensive cycle of tsalung, following empowerment, preliminary and generation stage practice (i.e. Dudjom Tersar).
Foundations of Tibetan Yoga intensives provide an opportunity for dharma practitioners and general populations training in traditional Buddhist yoga systems, to support their primary lineage practice, as well as an immersion into physical or energetic practices for health and to support meditation.
James Bae offers these programs in-person only. These programs are offered with a suggestion donation scale, and amenable to sangha needs as per location.
We are offering two upcoming programs:
Garchen Meditation Center, June 24-26
France, Sept 4-6. Donation plus the center’s lodging and meal costs (residential or commuter). Near Montpellier

10th – 11th & 17th–18th, OCTOBER 2026
In this four days divided into two weekends, only on Zoom, Geshe Choekhortshang Rinpoche will bestow the continuation of the preliminaries (started in 2025) for the Tummo practice from Ma Gyu and Kusum Rangshar lineages. We will experience the preparation retreat to achieve the basic level for the next part of Tummo practice in 2027.
Tummo practice is a very powerful, secret and ancient yogic practice which will allow you to use the inner heat to burn our karmic traces, our illness, our emotional afflictions and increase our body and mental well-being, our mental clarity, and our open-heartendness. Rinpoche will teach us how to use breath and movements of our body to increase the quality of our lives through this yogic and tantric tradition from Yungdrung Bon.

text:
Join us for a rare and profound three-day Dzogchen retreat in the sacred Valley of the Dakinis, Lincoln, Vermont.
The Drikung Dzogchen Community of Vermont warmly invites you to The Completion of Primordial Wisdom: A Yangzab Dzogchen Retreat with Ven. Khenpo Yeshi Rinpoche — a deeply trained scholar-practitioner whose path includes intensive Dzogchen retreat, decades of teaching in Nepal and the U.S., and a long connection with His Eminence Garchen Rinpoche.
The Yangzab treasure teachings are unique to the Drikung Kagyü lineage, revealed as an earth treasure by Drikung Tertön Rinchen Phuntsok.
Padmasambhava himself prophesied that these teachings would be especially effective at cutting through spiritual darkness in difficult times — swift and complete in leading to full realization.
✨
Over three days, Khenpo Rinpoche will guide us through The Completion of Primordial Wisdom, the root Dzogrim text of the Yangzab cycle, along with Padmasattva Guru Yoga and transmissions for the completion-stage practices of Trekchö and Thögal.
🏔
A holy place of practice. His Holiness Drikung Chetsang Rinpoche and other lamas have recognized this valley in Lincoln as a true power place for spiritual practice — a sacred land where the dakinis abide. It was here that His Holiness had a Peace Stupa built, and ever since His Holiness the 16th Karmapa first visited in 1977, well over thirty Tibetan teachers have come to this valley to turn the wheel of Dharma.
This year, the Drikung Dzogchen Community of Vermont celebrates its 25th anniversary of holding the Yangzab lineage here. Come help us mark this milestone in such a holy and blessed place.
🙏
Whether you join us in person at Sunray Peace Village or online from anywhere in the world, this is a rare opportunity to connect with an authentic living lineage in a land long devoted to awakening.
Our Vision
🌿
DDCV was envisioned as a bridge between traditions — preserving the integrity of each lineage while creating a living environment for practice, study, and realization in the 21st century, as held by the supreme Dharma lord Drikung Rinchen Phuntsok (1509–1557).
Our path weaves three streams into one: the Yangzab Dzogchen treasure teachings (the natural perfection of awareness), Drikung Kagyü Mahāmudrā (direct realization of the nature of mind), and Kadampa Lojong (mind-training and compassion in daily life). Through these, practitioners purify obscurations, open wisdom, and awaken uncontrived compassion.
"The DDCV stands for the inseparable union of view and conduct—where Dzogchen's vastness, Mahamudra's clarity, and Kadampa's discipline meet in one stream of living Dharma."
— H.E. Ontul Rinpoche, founder of DDCV

About the Teacher
Ven. Khenpo Yeshi Rinpoche was born in Nakchu, East Tibet, in 1969. He showed a deep devotion to the Dharma from early childhood, and at thirteen received empowerments, transmissions, and oral instructions in the Longchen Nyingthig cycle from His Eminence Lungtok Rinpoche and His Eminence Nidrag Khenpo Konchok Gyaltsen.
At twenty he traveled to South India, where he trained across the Geluk, Kagyü, and Nyingma traditions at Sera Monastery, Dzogchen Monastery, and Drikung Jangchup Ling, excelling in both sutra and tantra.

He studied closely with more than two dozen eminent masters and received the kama and terma transmissions of the Nyingma tradition from teachers including His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama, Tulku Urgyen Rinpoche, Chatral Sangye Dorje Rinpoche, and His Holiness Drikung Chetsang Rinpoche.
In 1993 he entered an intensive three-year retreat in Nepal under the renowned Dzogchen master Tulku Urgyen Rinpoche, who granted him special Dzogchen teachings and personal instructions at the Asura Cave Retreat Center in Pharping. He went on to teach at Ka-Nying Shedrup Ling and Rangjung Yeshe Institute, helping shape monastic and international curricula still in use today. His connection with His Eminence Garchen Rinpoche, beginning in 1998, eventually brought him to reside in the United States.
Dr. Khenpo Rinpoche holds a PhD in South and Southeast Asian Studies from UC Berkeley and is currently a postdoctoral fellow at the Ho Center for Buddhist Studies at Stanford University.
The combination of his deep retreat experience, breadth of lineage transmission, and scholarly depth makes the chance to receive these teachings directly from him a genuinely rare opportunity.

_Chagdud Khadro is the spiritual director of Chagdud Gonpa Brasil, Uruguay, and Chile, and head of Khadro Ling – the first Tibetan Buddhist centre in South America. Married for 23 years to Chagdud Rinpoche, she now upholds the spiritual lineages that he transmitted, is widely known for her teachings on preparing for death, and is passionate about imparting the dharma to children. In 2024, Chagdud Khadro was awarded the Khyentse Fellowship, Khyentse Foundation’s top honour, for her tireless contributions and commitment to the Buddhadharma._

Seven Treasures Retreat begins tomorrow, June 15, at Pema Osel Ling
Join us June 15–24 for the Collection of Seven Treasures Guru Rinpoche Retreat with H.E. Namgyal Dawa Rinpoche.
The retreat begins tomorrow with the Seven Treasures Empowerment, followed in the afternoon by a welcoming procession for H.E. Namgyal Dawa Rinpoche. Everyone is warmly invited to gather as we welcome Rinpoche back to Pema Osel Ling and begin this auspicious cycle of practice together.
Over the course of the retreat, participants will join in empowerment, oral transmissions, group practice, tsok, jinsek fire pujas, sacred dance, and the culmination of the retreat with siddhis and sacred dances on the final day.
🔥
Jinsek fire pujas are ritual offering practices using the fire element to accomplish enlightened activity swiftly and powerfully.
🌺
Sacred dance, ritual music, offerings, and repeated practice come together as part of this profound Vajrayana retreat centered on Guru Rinpoche and the blessings of the lineage.
All are welcome to attend in person or online. No one will be turned away due to lack of funds. Please contact office@vajrayana.org if you need assistance to attend.

Mudras in Mother Tantra
Khenpo Nyima Dhondup Rinpoche
July 10–12, 2026, Wilga and online
We invite you to a meditation course with an exceptional teacher—Khenpo Nyima Dhondup Rinpoche. Khenpo is one of the principal teachers at the Tibetan Menri Monastery. This will be his second visit to Poland. This time, he will be giving teachings on the Mother Tantra.
The Mother Tantra is one of the most important meditation texts in the Tibetan Bon tradition. This text encompasses many types of meditation; it is very unique and profound. Khenpo will explain the basic meditations based on this text and demonstrate specific mudras (hand positions) used in these meditations.
The course will be conducted in English with translation into Polish.
The course is open to everyone, including beginners.

𝐒𝐲𝐧𝐨𝐩𝐬𝐢𝐬: What you cling to will sink you, no matter how beautiful it appears. Let go, bail out the boat, and cross to the far shore by cultivating mindfulness in daily life (based on Snp. 4.1 𝐾𝑎𝑚𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑡𝑡𝑎 and Snp 5.1 𝐴𝑗𝑖𝑡ℎ𝑎𝑠𝑢𝑡𝑡𝑎)

Join us on Wednesday, June 24th for "Wandering and Meditating in Wild Places" by Dr. Julia Stenzel
The Dakinis’ Vast Resounding Expanse (mkha’ ’gro gad rgyangs) is the chöd practice of the Khandro Nyingthik tradition, revealed by the tertön Jigme Lingpa (1730–1798). This practice forms the basis for meditation in wild and liminal environments, known as nyensa (gnyan sa), a peripatetic meditation tradition in which practitioners are constantly on the move and meditate in caves, charnel grounds, and other traditionally consecrated or haunted landscapes. For a meditator on nyensa, the landscape itself becomes an active field of practice as opposed to the experience in a single bounded retreat site. Drawing on the 108-day nyensa practice of Khentrul Ngawang Lhundrup, a disciple of Chatral Rinpoche, in the spring of 2025, this lecture will first introduce three key texts that form the commentarial basis of mkha’ ‘gro gad rgyang. It will then present observations and reflections from Khentrul Ngawang Lhundrup’s journey, highlighting how doctrinal instructions are enacted through movement across diverse environments. Finally, it will address a broader question: What significance does meditative wandering through wilderness, charnel grounds, and other liminal or fear-inducing spaces hold for Buddhist practitioners in the twenty-first century?
Dr. Julia Stenzel is director of studies and associate professor at Rangjung Yeshe Institute, Kathmandu. She received her doctorate in Buddhist Studies from McGill University in Montreal, Canada. Her research examines ethics in Indian and Tibetan Mahāyāna Buddhism. Her doctoral research examined Buddhist compassion cultivation and its adaptation in secular compassion training programs.

text:
Dzogchen Mumed Dalpa
with Khenchen Tenpa Yungdrung Rinpoche
July 17 to August 5, 2026
In Shenten Dargye Ling and online
The Mume Dalpa is a foundational Dzogchen text within the Yungdrung Bön tradition. It presents the direct experience of the mind’s natural, non-conceptual state — and is beyond all intellectual thought. The term Mu-med means “beyond concept or duality,” and bdal-pa refers to vast "all-pervasiveness" or as well in terms as "primordial or pure awareness" (Rigpa).
This year’s Summer Retreat at Shenten Dargye Ling is guided by Khenchen Tenpa Yungdrung Rinpoche, who will continue teaching the profound Dzogchen text Mume Dalpa (Tib. Mu-med bdal-pa).
Following the inspiring beginning of this study and practice last year, we now continue our exploration of these essential teachings. New participants are most welcome. Rinpoche’s clear explanations and compassionate guidance make these teachings accessible to all sincere practitioners, whether familiar with Dzogchen or new to it.
The Mume Dalpa is a foundational Dzogchen text within the Yungdrung Bön tradition. It presents the direct experience of the mind’s natural, non-conceptual state – and is beyond all intellectual thought. The term Mu-med means “beyond concept or duality,” and bdal-pa refers to vast "all-pervasiveness" or, in terms of "primordial or pure awareness" (Rigpa).
During this retreat, participants will receive its teachings and, through meditations, be guided to recognise the essence of Dzogchen (the Great Perfection) in accordance with the Yungdrung Bön tradition.

Dr Choo co-founded Ninkatec, which provides home care for patients to lead fulfilling lives. He has been called the "Doctor-On-Wheels" and whole heartedly serves his patients with compassion, sincerity and care. He is also a certified Mindfulness Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) trainer. How do we face sickness with equanimity and wisdom? Find out from Dr Choo's wealth of compassion and experience!

July brings a full menu of wisdom, meditation, and movement. Whether you’re feeding your mind or stretching your body, there’s a space for you to grow with us!
𝐈𝐧𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐚𝐲𝐬

🌱
𝐒𝐮𝐧, 𝟓 𝐉𝐮𝐥, 𝟏𝟎 𝐀𝐌: “𝐾𝑒𝑒𝑝𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑅𝑜𝑏𝑒 𝐴𝑙𝑖𝑣𝑒 (𝑃𝑎𝑟𝑡 1)” by 𝐀𝐲𝐲𝐚 𝐔𝐩𝐞𝐤𝐤𝐡𝐚
What does monastic life look like in today’s world? Ayya Upekkha shares how we can support the monastic rules and sustain the Dhamma with courage.
🌱
𝐒𝐮𝐧, 𝟏𝟐 𝐉𝐮𝐥, 𝟏𝟎:𝟑𝟎 𝐀𝐌: "𝑆𝑎𝑣𝑜𝑢𝑟 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑁𝑖𝑘𝑎𝑦𝑎𝑠 – 𝑆𝑁 (𝑃𝑎𝑟𝑡 3)” by 𝐒𝐢𝐬 𝐒𝐲𝐥𝐯𝐢𝐚 𝐁𝐚𝐲
Back by popular demand
🗣️
! Join Sis Sylvia as she brings the Samyutta Nikaya to life with clarity, and her characteristic cheerful humour.
🌱
𝐒𝐮𝐧 𝟏𝟗 𝐉𝐮𝐥, 𝟏𝟎:𝟑𝟎 𝐀𝐌: " 𝑀𝑒𝑑𝑖𝑡𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛 𝑆𝑢𝑛𝑑𝑎𝑦” by 𝐁𝐫𝐨 𝐓𝐚𝐧 𝐁𝐞𝐧𝐠 𝐇𝐨𝐜𝐤
A full morning to just breathe. Let Bro Beng Hock guide you once again through stillness, calm, and the joy of a settled mind. We can't get enough of this!
🌱
𝐒𝐮𝐧 𝟐𝟔 𝐉𝐮𝐥, 𝟏𝟎 𝐀𝐌: “𝑊ℎ𝑎𝑡 𝑖𝑠 𝑡𝑜 𝑏𝑒 𝐴𝑏𝑎𝑛𝑑𝑜𝑛𝑒𝑑, 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐻𝑜𝑤?” by 𝐁𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐞 𝐒𝐞𝐞𝐥𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐚
We’re honoured to host Bhante Seelananda, who was born in Sri Lanka and is currently based in US, for 4 power-packed sessions.
Come for the Dhamma, stay for the community. Let’s make July a month of clear minds, kind hearts, and happy steps together.

We are delighted to announce a very special event hosted by Rangjung Yeshe Gomde Pyrénées onsite and online:
Teachings by Kyabjé Khen Rinpoche Tashi Palden
(Kyabjé Khenpo)
On Heart Sutra
25th July to 3rd August 2026 -  11am CET
Onsite and Online
The Heart Sutra, Sanskrit Prajnaparamitahridaya-sutra (“Discourse on the Heart of the Perfection of Wisdom”), in Mahayana Buddhism, an extremely brief yet highly influential distillation of the essence of (“Perfection of Wisdom”) writings.

It is both rare and precious for a master like His Eminence Jhado Rinpoche to come to the UK and turn the wheel of Dharma.
This September, at the request of his students here and as part of his wider European tour, Rinpoche comes to London for five days of teaching, from Wednesday 9 to Sunday 13 September.
It is a programme of depth, moving through the breadth of the Tibetan Buddhist path:
Days 1 & 2 (9–10 Sept): Kalachakra initiation, with teachings on the Three Principal Aspects of the Path and Lojong (mind training)
Day 3 (11 Sept): Cittamani Tara initiation and oral transmission
Days 4 & 5 (12–13 Sept): Lama Tsongkhapa and Medicine Buddha jenangs, with commentary on the Lama Chöpa (Guru Puja)
The programme is designed so students and practitioners can attend all five days, or you can select:
- days 1 and 2 together
- just day 3
- days 4 and 5 together
- any combination thereof
Rinpoche's UK visit is hosted in conjunction with our friends at Jamyang Leeds and Land of Joy.
Registration opens Wednesday 1 July. Hold the dates, and we will share the booking link as soon as it is live; the chance to sit with a teacher of this calibre is not one that comes around often.
https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdsbLFzb6X7bi_to-73gXX80s2Aue40YgacjXZ8NuRBg5aqQg/viewform

text:
Online Gegsel Retreat
– with Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche
Yoga from the Sky Dharma Tradition
Friday, July 3, 2026 – Sunday, July 5, 2026
Each day
5:00 pm – 8:00 pm CET, 11:00 pm – 2:00 am MYT
Language: English with simultaneous German and Russian translation
Contribution: 60 €
No previous knowledge is required, everyone is welcome!
Registration:
https://us02web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_yVMddQ6HSk6mcgPs_-m8Uw#/registration
After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the webinar. If you do not have a Paypal account, please register with Carmen (carmen@dolpotulku.org) and transfer the fee to our association account.
We warmly invite you to join Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche for the Nam Chö Tsa Lung Gegsel Online Retreat and discover an authentic yoga practice from the Nam Chö (Sky Dharma) tradition.
Gegsel, meaning "overcoming obstacles," combines movement, breath control, visualization, and meditation to release physical tension, emotional stress, and energetic blockages while cultivating greater balance, vitality, and inner clarity. The Namchö Tsa Lung Gegsel exercises have been passed down in unbroken lineage from Tertön (treasure finder) Tulku Mingyur Dorje (1645–1667) to Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche.
Although Gegsel is traditionally taught alongside Tummo practice, no previous experience is required. Whether you are completely new to Tibetan yoga or already have an established meditation practice, this retreat offers a wonderful opportunity to experience these profound methods in an accessible and practical way.
Schedule each day:
5:00 pm – 6:30 pm CET teaching and practice by Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche
7:00 pm – 8:00 pm CET practice tutorial
You can find more information about Dolpo Tulku Rinpoche's teachings and activities on our website:
https://www.dolpotulku.org/en/program/

Lopon Drukdra Gyatso serves as Retreat Master of Kinzang Choling Three-Year Retreat Centre — the principal retreat centre for graduates of Gangteng Buddhist University, under the guidance of His Holiness Gangteng Rinpoche. text:
Gosia is offering an ongoing series of Tummo classes with a particular emphasis on an embodied and intuitive approach to Highest Yoga Tantra. This will be a safe and nurturing space — especially welcoming to female practitioners — for those who wish to explore a more experiential and embodied style of HYT practice, encompassing both the Generation and Completion Stages.
📅
Beginning on Friday, July 10th, at 3pm Eastern Time / 8pm UK time, we will gather every week at the same day and time for guided practice followed by Q&A. This new cycle will focus on Vajrayogini Generation Stage practice alongside the Completion Stage practices of the Six Yogas of Naropa, and will have a rich, community-centred format that encourages connection and mutual support.
⏺️
Sessions can be attended live or accessed through recordings, depending on what your week allows.
🫂
The heart of this offering is the creation of a community of intuitive practitioners who walk the path together — supporting one another on the journey toward Enlightenment for the benefit of all beings. We each carry unique gifts to share, and it is in that spirit of open exchange that wisdom, compassion, and genuine breakthroughs arise naturally, held within an atmosphere of kindness and mutual respect.
🧘‍♀️
🧘
Looking forward to seeing you in the Dakini Land community.
🙏
You can register here:
https://dakini-land.circle.so/checkout/dakini-tummo
📿
📿
📿
About Gosia:
✨
Gosia has been a dedicated practitioner of Vajrayana Buddhism for twenty years, and is based in the south-west of Ireland. A sociologist and psychotherapist by training, she brings a deep passion for discovering and expanding human potential — both within herself and in those she works with.
🙏
Gosia has received the transmission and authorisation from her teachers  — Lama Jhampa Shaneman and Lama Glenn Mullin — to teach and transmit Tantra. This includes the Tummo practices of the Six Yogas of Naropa and Niguma, Chittamani Tara, the Amitayus Healing Tummo, and a range of other Vajrayana practices.
See you soon,
🪷
Gosia

text:
Due to several requests, I will give the lung/oral transmission and tri/commentary for Dudjom Rinpoche's very special, extremely short ngon-dro on the most holy day of Cho-kor Du-chen:
https://www.lotsawahouse.org/tibetan-masters/dudjom-rinpoche/key-summary-ngondro-main?fbclid=IwY2xjawS0pj5leHRuA2FlbQIxMQBzcnRjBmFwcF9pZBAyMjIwMzkxNzg4MjAwODkyAAEe-u-Y8RudgX6eafXvjMFJqHotIb1TPBEw-cAxe1eNHd9NnzH658vM6zhpD6A_aem_9l7UIAwjt-yWodftynvY0Q
This ngon-dro was requested from Kyabje Dudjom Rinpoche by Jetsun Pema Chodzin Sangngag Wangmo, the reincarnation of Jetsun Shugseb Chonyid Wangmo, when Jetsun Pema Rinpoche and Her Family, Mr. & Mrs. Sonam T. Kazi, were preparing to move to NYC to teach a group of Western students in 1970. In the colophon, it says that this ngon-dro was specifically composed for busy modern people. As written, it does not include prostrations, just the five accumulations of refuge, Bodhicitta, Vajrasattva, mandala offering, and Vajra Guru mantra. In retreat, these accumulations can be done in 15-21 days. I received this ngon-dro from Mr. Kazi in Delhi in 1970 and again from Kyabje Dudjom Rinpoche in 1972. I've been doing this ngon-dro for 56 years.
Date: Saturday, July 18, 8 AM MDT
Duration: 90 minutes
Join Zoom Meeting
https://us02web.zoom.us/j/83278902405?pwd=ktXgHGbCqZmoDa9U4uCoVSbc9KIF6y.1
Meeting chat link
https://us02web.zoom.us/launch/jc/83278902405
Meeting ID: 832 7890 2405
Passcode: 341439
Prerequisite: The samaya or promise to complete 111,111 recitations of each of the five accumulations within a maximum of one year from the date of the transmission.
This transmission is only for those who seriously want to practice this ngon-dro. It is not for Dharma "stamp-collectors." Please do not attend this transmission unless you are ready and willing to accept this samaya.
Dana: The suggested minimum thanksgiving offering is $100.  This is about Enlightenment, not some worldly benefit. As such, this sadhana is incredibly precious. An offering of $1000, $10,000, $100,000, or even $1,000,000 would not be too much if one understands its true value. I spent six months traveling back and forth to India overland twice in order to receive this sadhana.  All offerings for this lung will, in turn, be offered to the building of the Vajrakilaya Peace Mandala at Phurba Thrinley Ling above the Mississippi River in Lansing, IA. This has nothing to do with me or my economy. It has to do with how precious this very special ngon-dro is.
Offerings may be made via Paypal:
@lamapc13
or Venmo:
@lamapema13
Recipients of this transmission may ask me on-going questions about its practice by emailing me at: 13pemachophel@gmail.com. Depending on interest and need, I am open to scheduling further Zoom classes on instructions on this practice. I make the commitment to helping those who receive this ngon-dro to complete it in whatever ways I can.
After this transmission is complete, those who have received it may email me for Mr. Kazi's and my translation of it.
